issues.display_title: Issue #1682: Optimize Functions in Layouts Component
issues.main_description_html:
Goal

Optimize layouts.component.ts functions without changing the flow (clean code).

Tasks

  • Replace Manual Unsubscribe: Use takeUntilDestroyed for _userInfoSub & _ownProfileWaitSub. Remove ngOnDestroy.
  • Deduplicate Google Auth Init: Extract google.accounts.id.initialize to a shared function.
  • Refactor handleVerifedAccount: Convert nested promise/subscribe into a cleaner RxJS / async-await flow.
  • Refactor DOM Access: Use safe chaining for document.querySelector & getElementById.

issues.display_title: Issue #1683: Optimize Functions in Header Component
issues.main_description_html:
Goal

Optimize header.component.ts functions without changing the flow. Prevent memory leaks and improve scroll performance.

Tasks

  • Fix Memory Leaks: Add DestroyRef.onDestroy() to clean up event listeners in initScrollShadow and initMobileMenuListeners.
  • Optimize Scroll Spy: Cache DOM elements (.nav and section IDs) in attachSectionObserver.
  • Refactor DOM Access: Use @Inject(DOCUMENT) instead of the global document object.
  • Clean Subscriptions: Ensure all component subscriptions and listeners are properly destroyed.

issues.display_title: Issue #1684: Optimize Functions in Profile Main Component
issues.main_description_html:
Goal

Optimize profile/main/main.component.ts. Clean up deadcode, fix memory leaks, and modernize deprecated APIs.

Tasks

  • Fix Severe Memory Leak: Store setInterval IDs created in countdown() and clear them all in ngOnDestroy().
  • Modernize Deprecated API: Replace document.execCommand('copy') with navigator.clipboard.writeText() in copyText().
  • Refactor DOM Access: Use @Inject(DOCUMENT) instead of the global document.
  • Clean Up RxJS & Recursion: Replace manual recursive calls for TimeoutError in getExhibitions() and deleteExhibition() with RxJS retry() or bound the retries.
  • Clean Code: Simplify openLink() and countdown() logic.

Contact Component Optimization Design

Goal

Deconstruct the massive contact.component.ts (742 lines) into 3 independent child components. The main component will only handle the outer layout, accordion state, and scroll animations, while the child components will handle their respective forms, validations, recaptcha, and API submissions.

Architecture

Parent Component:

  • ContactComponent: Manages only the outer UI layout, accordion state, and scroll reveal animations.

Child Components (New):

  • ContactGeneralComponent: Manages the UI, recaptcha, & form submission for "General Inquiry".
  • ContactHelpComponent: Manages the UI, file uploads, recaptcha, & form submission for "Help & Support".
  • ContactCustomComponent: Manages the UI, file uploads, recaptcha, & form submission for "Custom Request".

Service:

  • Recaptcha logic, file validation, and API calls will continue using the existing services (ContactService, MainService), injected directly into each child component.

Data Flow

  • Child components call the API via MainService.postContactUs.
  • Child components independently manage their loading, success, and progress states.
  • The parent component does not need to know the form details. It only passes context/inputs when necessary.
  • The parent component notifies the child when the accordion is opened so the child can load the recaptcha.

Verification

  1. The form UIs render perfectly.
  2. The submission for each form is successfully saved.
  3. File validation and recaptcha are functioning properly.
  4. ResizeObserver is properly cleaned up on destroy.

Profile Setting Component Optimization Design

Goal

Refactor profile-setting.component.ts (476 lines) by extracting the complex and verbose avatar image cropping and uploading logic into a dedicated component.

Architecture

Parent Component:

  • ProfileSettingComponent: Focuses on managing the main user profile data form (first name, last name, phone, etc.), handling main API submissions, and tracking component state.

Child Component (New):

  • AvatarUploadComponent: Specifically handles the profile picture feature. This includes file selection, validation (size/format), image cropping (via ngx-image-cropper), and API upload to AWS S3.

Data Flow

  • ProfileSettingComponent passes the initial profile.avatar URL as an @Input to AvatarUploadComponent.
  • AvatarUploadComponent internally manages all the cropping UI states and progress indicators.
  • When an upload succeeds, AvatarUploadComponent emits an @Output event (avatarUpdated) containing the new image URL back to the parent.
  • The parent component simply updates its state with the new URL without worrying about the upload mechanism.

Verification

  1. The user profile data form can still be saved correctly.
  2. Avatar image cropping and uploading work flawlessly without errors.
  3. The newly uploaded avatar URL is correctly emitted back to the parent and displayed.
